name: Trade & Customs official sources
notes: Trade & Customs carries HIGH-stakes public numbers: import duty rates, CET bands, VAT and levies, AfCFTA/regional preference offers and rules-of-origin tests. A wrong duty rate or a falsely 'free' preference misprices a shipment and can trigger a customs penalty.

The duty datasets (data/trade/country-duty-rates.js, landed-cost-data.js) carry no per-figure date. datasetReviewed above is a floor, not a per-rate date. Treat any duty/levy/VAT figure older than highRiskCadenceDays as planning-grade and confirm with the named authority before pricing.

Never change a duty rate, CET band, VAT rate or levy because a URL's content hash changed. Open the tariff schedule or gazette and read it, exactly as .claude/rules/government.md requires. A national budget/finance act is the usual trigger for a real change.

A national customs authority outranks any aggregator, freight forwarder or broker page for the duty/VAT/levy claim of its own country. A regional secretariat (AfCFTA, ECOWAS, EAC, SADC, COMESA) is authoritative for the bloc's CET bands, preference schedule and rules of origin; the national authority applies them.

15 of the 24 duty-rate markets have a bound customs-authority URL today. The other 9 are recorded in gaps.authoritiesWithoutUrl as known liabilities, not to-dos. Do not delete one without either finding the source or showing the figure as planning-grade.

Shipping/freight rates, port demurrage tariffs, LC bank fees, B2B payment-provider fees, trade-finance instrument costs and commodity spot prices are COMMERCIAL market data, not regulator-published. They are recorded in gaps.unsourcedClaims by design and must never be presented as an official rate.

The bug class to watch, per .claude/rules/energy.md and .claude/rules/telecom.md: `country.data.field || 0` (or `|| 1` on an FX rate) turns missing data into free/wrong, which then wins a cost comparison. Force a user-entered value or filter the dropdown to covered markets — never default a missing rate to zero. Any dropdown that offers more markets than a dataset covers is the tell.

sources.title: Nigeria Customs Service — tariff & import duty
sources.notes: Customs authority for Nigeria. Authoritative for import duty, VAT (7.5%), CISS/ETLS/surcharge levies and ECOWAS CET application. Read the current tariff/gazette or Finance Act change before altering a rate — a changed page is not a rate change.

sources.title: Kenya Revenue Authority — customs & border control
sources.notes: Customs authority for Kenya. Authoritative for import duty, VAT (16%), IDF/RDL levies and EAC CET application. Read the current EAC gazette or Finance Act before altering a rate.

sources.title: SARS Customs — tariff & import
sources.notes: Customs authority for South Africa (SACU). Authoritative for import duty, VAT (15%) and SACU/SADC treatment. ITAC sets the tariff; SARS administers it. Read the published tariff before altering a rate.

sources.title: Ghana Revenue Authority — Customs
sources.notes: Customs authority for Ghana. Authoritative for import duty, VAT (15%), NHIL/GETFund/AU/ECOWAS/exam/processing levies and ECOWAS CET application. Read the current tariff/gazette before altering a rate.

sources.title: Tanzania Revenue Authority — customs
sources.notes: Customs authority for Tanzania. Authoritative for import duty, VAT (18%), IDF/RDL levies and EAC CET application. Read the current EAC gazette or Finance Act before altering a rate.

sources.title: Uganda Revenue Authority — customs
sources.notes: Customs authority for Uganda. Authoritative for import duty, VAT (18%) and EAC CET application. Read the current EAC gazette or Finance Act before altering a rate.

sources.title: Rwanda Revenue Authority — customs
sources.notes: Customs authority for Rwanda. Authoritative for import duty, VAT (18%), IDSL/AU levies and EAC CET application. Read the current EAC gazette or Finance Act before altering a rate.

sources.title: Zambia Revenue Authority — customs
sources.notes: Customs authority for Zambia (COMESA/SADC). Authoritative for import duty and VAT. Read the current tariff/gazette before altering a rate.

sources.title: Zimbabwe Revenue Authority — customs
sources.notes: Customs authority for Zimbabwe (COMESA/SADC). Authoritative for import duty and VAT. Read the current tariff/gazette before altering a rate.

sources.title: Egyptian Customs Authority — Ministry of Finance
sources.notes: Customs authority for Egypt (under Ministry of Finance). Authoritative for import duty, VAT (14%) and services fee. Verified live 2026-07. Read the current tariff/decree before altering a rate.

sources.title: Douanes Sénégalaises
sources.notes: Customs authority for Senegal (ECOWAS/WAEMU). Authoritative for import duty, VAT, PCS/PCO levies and ECOWAS CET application. Verified live 2026-07. Read the current WAEMU/ECOWAS tariff before altering a rate.

sources.authority: DGD (Direction Générale des Douanes de Côte d'Ivoire)
sources.title: Douanes Ivoiriennes
sources.notes: Customs authority for Côte d'Ivoire (ECOWAS/WAEMU). Authoritative for import duty, VAT (18%), PCS/PCO/statistical levies and ECOWAS CET application. Verified live 2026-07. Read the current WAEMU/ECOWAS tariff before altering a rate.

sources.authority: ADII (Administration des Douanes et Impôts Indirects)
sources.title: Douane Maroc — ADII
sources.notes: Customs authority for Morocco. Authoritative for import duty and VAT (20%). Portal is behind a WAF that rejected an automated fetch 2026-07 — treat as manual-review, not as evidence the tariff is unchanged. douane.gov.ma is the documented official ADII domain.

sources.title: Portail officiel de la Douane Tunisienne
sources.notes: Customs authority for Tunisia (GAFTA). Authoritative for import duty and VAT. Verified live 2026-07. Read the current tariff before altering a rate.

sources.title: Douanes Algériennes
sources.notes: Customs authority for Algeria (GAFTA/AU). Authoritative for import duty and VAT. Verified live 2026-07. Read the current tariff before altering a rate.

sources.title: AfCFTA — African Continental Free Trade Area
sources.notes: Authoritative body for AfCFTA tariff-liberalisation schedules, rules of origin and the Guided Trade Initiative. A preferential-duty claim still depends on the exact national tariff offer, product classification and origin proof — the tracker presents schedule stages, not a legal ruling.

sources.title: ECOWAS — Common External Tariff & ETLS
sources.notes: Authoritative for the ECOWAS 5-band CET and the ETLS duty-free scheme. National customs authorities (NCS, GRA, DGD) apply the CET and add national levies; confirm the band and any national surcharge with the destination authority.

sources.title: EAC — Common External Tariff
sources.notes: Authoritative for the EAC CET bands and the sensitive-items list (e.g. sugar, rice). The annual EAC Gazette carries stays of application and duty-remission changes — read it before altering a band. National revenue authorities (KRA, TRA, URA, RRA) apply the CET.

sources.title: SADC — Trade Protocol & Rules of Origin
sources.notes: Authoritative for the SADC Trade Protocol and product-specific rules of origin. The rules-of-origin checker prepares the evidence trail; confirm the product-specific rule with the competent authority or a licensed broker before claiming preference.

sources.title: COMESA — Free Trade Area & Rules of Origin
sources.notes: Authoritative for the COMESA FTA preference and rules of origin used on the COMESA Certificate of Origin. Confirm the product rule with the destination authority before claiming preference.

sources.title: WCO — Harmonized System nomenclature
sources.notes: Authoritative for the 6-digit Harmonized System nomenclature. National tariff lines extend HS to 8–10 digits and set the duty rate; the HS lookup classifies, it does not quote a legal duty. The HS is revised on a multi-year cycle (HS 2022, HS 2027).

authoritiesWithoutUrl.note: Ethiopia's revenue body was split; the customs function moved to the Ethiopian Customs Commission. The duty rates are a bundled planning default with no bound URL — find the current Commission tariff, or show the figure as planning-grade.
Cameroon (CEMAC) duty/VAT figures are a bundled planning default with no bound URL — find the DGD Cameroun / CEMAC CET tariff, or show the figure as planning-grade.
Angola (SADC) duty/VAT figures are a bundled planning default with no bound URL — find the AGT pauta aduaneira, or show the figure as planning-grade.
Libya duty figures are a bundled planning default with no bound URL and a limited regulatory framework — show the figure as planning-grade.
Sudan duty figures are a bundled planning default with no bound URL — show the figure as planning-grade.
Mozambique (SADC) duty/VAT figures are a bundled planning default with no bound URL — find the AT pauta aduaneira, or show the figure as planning-grade.

authoritiesWithoutUrl.authority: Direction Générale des Douanes de Madagascar
authoritiesWithoutUrl.note: Madagascar (COMESA/SADC) duty/VAT figures are a bundled planning default with no bound URL — show the figure as planning-grade.

authoritiesWithoutUrl.authority: DGD Burkina Faso (Direction Générale des Douanes)
authoritiesWithoutUrl.note: Burkina Faso (ECOWAS/WAEMU) duty figures are a bundled planning default with no bound URL — find the WAEMU/ECOWAS CET application, or show the figure as planning-grade.
Mali (ECOWAS/WAEMU) duty figures are a bundled planning default with no bound URL — find the WAEMU/ECOWAS CET application, or show the figure as planning-grade.

unsourcedClaims.why: The per-HS-chapter 'typical' duty is an editable planning proxy, not a legal tariff line. National tariffs run to 8–10 digits with exemptions and product notes. The tools present a duty-rate INPUT the user overrides from the destination authority; the bundled figure is a starting point, never a ruling.

unsourcedClaims.field: VAT base composition (NG) — landed-cost vs ecowas-levy divergence
unsourcedClaims.markets: NG (check the other levy markets before trusting either)
unsourcedClaims.why: The two tools compute Nigeria's VAT base differently. landed-cost (engines/src/landed-cost-engine.js) uses CIF + duty + ALL levies (CISS + ETLS + surcharge). ecowas-levy uses CIF + duty + community levy + statistical fee only, excluding CISS/ETLS/surcharge — on CIF 10,000 at the 20% band that is a VAT base of 12,150 rather than ~12,435. Both are planning approximations; neither is bound to an NCS ruling on the statutory VAT base. Do NOT reconcile them by picking one — read the NCS tariff/VAT guidance first, per this ledger's rules, then align both engines in one change. Recorded 2026-07-17.

unsourcedClaims.field: ecowas-levy FOB derivation for FOB-based levies (CISS)
unsourcedClaims.why: ecowas-levy derives FOB from CIF at a fixed 95% (freight+insurance assumed 5%) to apply FOB-based levies such as Nigeria's 1% CISS. That is a modelling assumption, not a customs rule — the real FOB comes off the commercial invoice. Treat the CISS figure as indicative and confirm against the actual FOB.

Freight rates are commercial carrier quotes that move with fuel, capacity, season and GRI. No authority publishes them. Recorded as dated planning references; the estimator is a budgeting aid, not a quote.

Demurrage/storage tariffs are set by each terminal operator and shipping line, revised commercially. Recorded as planning references; confirm the current free-days and daily band with the line and terminal.

unsourcedClaims.field: lc-calculator bank fees / trade-finance-data instrument costs
unsourcedClaims.why: LC opening/confirmation/amendment/SWIFT fees and trade-finance instrument spreads are per-bank commercial pricing, not a published rate. Recorded as indicative planning ranges; confirm with the issuing/confirming bank.

Cross-border payment fees and FX spreads are provider commercial pricing that changes without notice and by corridor. Recorded as indicative; confirm live with the provider before quoting a buyer. PAPSS availability is corridor- and bank-dependent.
Commodity prices are market spot data and trade balances are national-statistics snapshots; both are perishable and recorded as dated references, not live feeds.

FX is market data reviewed 2026-03 in the bundled dataset. The tools pre-fill the latest bundled average and let the user override with a live rate; a missing currency must force user input, never default the rate to 1.
